prompt: Code review on inputs/eval-1-wrong-way-dependency/internal/order/order.go (Go, layout is internal/ + cmd/). Something feels off about the Order type depending on the database directly. Is this structured right? If not, what would you change?

assertions.text: Identifies that database/sql I/O inside the domain Order type is a smell — the reason points outward at a concrete
Recommends a repository-style interface (port) owned by the domain/reason
Moves the SQL into an adapter/connection (e.g. internal/postgres), leaving the domain free of database/sql
Keeps the order-total business rule in the domain, not pushed into the adapter
Notes the construction/wiring belongs in cmd (how it's run)
